ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-19-2022 Continued To Aug-16-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Pierson, via Zoom, specially appearing for Patanisha Davis-Jenkiins, for Carmen Howell and Sherrie Guillory, Co-Executors.

The court reminds counsel that the Co-Executors were appointed almost two years ago and points to the lack of movement since then. The court advises counsel that it needs a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Aug-16-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to file either of the petitions mentioned above by Aug-02-2022.

Judge: Hon. Cecilia Castellanos. Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Jan-27-2021 Continued To Mar-10-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-03-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patanisha Davis-Jenkins, Esq., counsel for and with Carmen Howell, Petitioner, and Sherrie Guillory (appearing by telephone).

At the outset, the Court queries Ms. Guillory as to her current telephone number and as to whether that number is her permanent home number. Ms. Guillory confirms the same. Court acknowledges concerns with the appointment of co-executors in light of certain issues that arose during the administration of the decedent's conservatorship. In that regard, both Ms. Howell and Ms. Guillory note that waivers of accountings have been filed in the conservatorship estate, a prerequisite for the co-executor proposition now before the Court.

At the Court's query, Ms. Howell states that the original will was in her possession many years ago but believes that the will was lost during a 1999 move to her current residence. The Court notes that based upon these and other representations by the parties, the Court finds that the presumptions of Probate Code Sec. 6124 are inapplicable in that as the will was not in the possession of the testator, it is not presumed to have been destroyed by her.

The Court admits into probate the copy of the lost will dated December 29, 1994.

The Court will take the current matter under submission but intends to grant the same appointing Ms. Howell and Ms. Guillory co-executors of the Estate of Dolores Ollison. The Court sets status hearing on the filing of the Inventory and Appraisal for December 28, 2020 and sets status for the close of the estate for August 25, 2021.

Ms. Davis-Jenkins is to prepare a new Proposed Order attaching a copy of the lost will to the Order.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-01-2020 Continued To Aug-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patanisha Davis-Jenkins appeared by telephone for and with Carmen Howell (also by telephone).

Ms. Davis-Jenkins stated that Ms. Guillory was not present on the phone line. The Court spoke of presumption of revocation under Probate Code 6124, stated that it does not see any meaningful discussion about capacity other than the Decedent was under a conservatorship, that being conserved doesn't necessarily mean someone does not have capacity and that there is no duplicate original of the will. Ms. Davis-Jenkins stated that the will was not in the possession of the Decedent, that the original will was last given to her Client for safe keeping when the will was drafted over 20-years ago and that her Client can't find it. The Court informed Ms. Davis-Jenkins that she needs to file something regarding rebuttal of revocation. The Court raised concern about appointing someone as a personal representative that has no phone and the Court not having the ability to contact Ms. Guillory. Ms. Davis-Jenkins stated that she can provide Ms. Guillory's Daughter's phone number. The Court stated that it will need to have contact with Ms. Guillory in order to make a finding that Ms. Guillory is able to effectively represent the estate. The Court also expressed concern about Ms. Davis-Jenkins and Ms. Howell working efficiently on administration of this estate given the history of their relationship in the conservatorship matter. Ms. Davis-Jenkins and Ms. Howell assured the Court that they are now working in harmony and that they are capable and ready to work together in administering the estate. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to August 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Davis-Jenkins is to file the following by August 17, 2020: 1) something that provides evidence regarding presumption of revocation and rebuttal of revocation, 2) something in support of Ms. Guillory being an appropriate representative of the estate, and 3) something indicating that the relationship of Ms. Davis-Jenkins and Ms. Howell will provide a way to effectively represent the estate.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguers. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
